title: Discord delivery failures are reported as successful
reasoning: The route does not inspect the webhook response before returning success. fetch resolves for HTTP error statuses, so Discord-side rejections such as 400, 401/403, 429, or 5xx are surfaced to the client as {success:true}. Users will believe a support report was submitted even when it was dropped by the downstream service.
reproduction: Configure SUPPORT_DISCORD_WEBHOOK to an endpoint that returns HTTP 429 or 500, POST any valid JSON body to /api/support, and observe that the API still responds 200 with {"success":true}.
recommendation: Capture the fetch response, check response.ok, log the status/body as appropriate, and return a non-2xx or retryable error response when Discord rejects the message.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests exercise the support route or simulate non-2xx webhook responses.
suggestedRegressionTest: Mock global fetch to resolve with { ok: false, status: 429 } and assert POST returns a failure status/body instead of success:true.
